From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

(Redirected from [European Anticapitalist Left](/w/index.php?title=European_Anticapitalist_Left&redirect=no "European Anticapitalist Left"))

Former anti-capitalist European political alliance

| European Anti-Capitalist Left | |
| --- | --- |
|  | |
| President | Collective leadership |
| Founded | March 2000 (2000-03) |
| Dissolved | 2012 (2012)[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*] |
| Headquarters | [Brussels](/wiki/Brussels "Brussels") |
| [Ideology](/wiki/List_of_political_ideologies "List of political ideologies") | [Anti-capitalism](/wiki/Anti-capitalism "Anti-capitalism") [Socialism](/wiki/Socialism "Socialism") [Soft Euroscepticism](/wiki/Soft_Euroscepticism "Soft Euroscepticism") |
| [Political position](/wiki/Political_spectrum "Political spectrum") | [Left-wing](/wiki/Left-wing_politics "Left-wing politics") |
| [European Parliament group](/wiki/Political_groups_of_the_European_Parliament "Political groups of the European Parliament") | [The Left in the European Parliament](/wiki/The_Left_in_the_European_Parliament "The Left in the European Parliament") |
| Colours | [Red](/wiki/Red "Red"), [Black](/wiki/Black "Black"), [White](/wiki/White "White"), [Pink](/wiki/Pink "Pink") |
| * [Politics of the European Union](/wiki/Politics_of_the_European_Union "Politics of the European Union") * [Political parties](/wiki/List_of_political_parties_in_the_European_Union "List of political parties in the European Union") * [Elections](/wiki/Elections_in_the_European_Union "Elections in the European Union") | |

The **European Anti-Capitalist Left** (**EACL**) was a [left-wing](/wiki/Left-wing_politics "Left-wing politics") [European political alliance](/wiki/European_political_alliance "European political alliance").

The EACL took part in the Europe against Austerity Conference in 2011 and 2012.[[1]](#cite_note-1)[[2]](#cite_note-2)

## Ideology

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=European_Anti-Capitalist_Left&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Ideology")]

Parties participating in the EACL are united in opposition to [neo-liberalism](/wiki/Neo-liberalism "Neo-liberalism") and [capitalism](/wiki/Capitalism "Capitalism"). The criteria for participation in its conferences are:

* A clear foundation in [anti-capitalism](/wiki/Anti-capitalism "Anti-capitalism"), with the goal of achieving [socialism](/wiki/Socialism "Socialism").
* Recognition of the pluralist nature of the left, with no claim of a monopoly for their own organisation or viewpoint.
* Pursuit of common action of the left in general coalitions.
* Some degree of representation (in local, regional, or national legislatures) and anchoring in social movements.

Despite the last criterion, many organisations involved in the EACL, with the exception of the founding parties, are somewhat marginal groups, often of [Trotskyist](/wiki/Trotskyism "Trotskyism") or [Marxist–Leninist anti-revisionist](/wiki/Anti-revisionism_(Marxism%E2%80%93Leninism) "Anti-revisionism (Marxism–Leninism)") persuasion which organize most vigorously through social movements and trade unions, rather than through electoral processes. The EACL emphasises an organizational need to carry out a great deal of [extra-parliamentary activism](/wiki/Extra-parliamentary_opposition "Extra-parliamentary opposition").[[3]](#cite_note-3)
